title: Allow assigning events
body:
Currently Verilator disallows event assignements.

This PR aims to improve Verilator's capability in this regard. However, given my understanding of the complexity behind the current restrictions regarding events, I feel it's safe to say that a full support for event passing/assignements is a long way to go.

So, I'm focusing on cases where _dynamic triggger scheduler_ is used (`VlDynamicTriggerScheduler __VldynShed`), as it's the only one that seems to be designed in a way that would allow this to work. Any trigger evaluation that involved the active evaluation loop is doomed at the moment for the following reason:
1. Events are assumed to be statically allocated and the following properties of the generated code require this:
    1. Active eval loop calls directly to a queue associated with the event
    2. The events get packed into a bit vector and their _triggered_ state checked with const bitmasks.
    3. Processes that await call directly to queues associated with the events.

Moreover, I think that multithreading support is something to be considered later, as this is a whole other can of worms. For now I think that the best assumption is that event assignments => no multithreading. As the whole static partitioning falls apart when we allow events to be passed around. Maybe it's possible to improve it with some graph-based algorithm, or maybe the best solution is to assume that events crossing between threads won't happen to often and just use mutexes. Maybe in case of events handled by dynamic scheduler it doesn't even affect the partitioning. I don't know.

Also, I don't want to start with support for _event merging_. Apparently even commercial simulators have problems with that bizarre feature.

--------

With those restrictions in mind, enabling passing the events around seems to be within our reach with no need for major overhaul of scheduling code. The downside is that it creates an extra layer of indirection in the runtime. If we want to avoid doing that I think we would have to design some kind of alogrithm that tries to detect which events might require that (Probably all events to which we assign and all events which get assigned to something), provide two different implementations for events and schedulers (one for direct access, another for indirect) and choose the adequate one in a given scenario. Another, simpler option is to pick the implementation globally, depending on whether the System Verilog code contains any event assignements or not, which might be a good next step forward I guess.

--------

I hope this will spark some discussion on how we can possibly take this further and what other things we might need to consider.

As for now:

TODO:

- [x] Allow passing events around
- [x] Create regression tests for this feature
- [x] Limit the event assignements only to cases where the _dynamic trigger scheduler_ would be used
- [x] ~~Disallow(?) multithreading / Warn on event-assignements~~
- [x] Minimize runtime penalty by limiting the amount of dynamic allocations and indirect access to event data.*

##### *At least to a degree that  won't hurt the runtime performance of code that Verilator can handle already.
